column623:
"Lets start looking at restoration

*RESTORATION:*  
1. *Validate backups* – Check all saved copies of your data to make sure they’re not infected by the attack. Only use backups from before the attack started.
2. *Wipe infected systems* – Completely erase (format) any computers or servers that were hit by the attack so no harmful files remain.
3. *Rebuild from clean backup* – Use the safe backup you found earlier to put the system back the way it was before the attack.
4. *Re-enable security tools on servers* – Before putting the systems back on the network
column626: and other security software so they’re protected.\n5. *Reconnect traffic to services and monitor* – Turn the systems back on
column628: and keep a close eye for anything suspicious.\"\n140

column790:
"Lets make sure we're following steps in order:

1. *alidate backups* – Check all saved copies of your data to make sure they’re not infected by the attack. Only use backups from before the attack started.
2. *Wipe infected systems* – Completely erase (format) any computers or servers that were hit by the attack so no harmful files remain.
3. *Rebuild from clean backup* – Use the safe backup you found earlier to put the system back the way it was before the attack.
4. *Re-enable security tools on servers* – Before putting the systems back on the network
column793: and other security software so they’re protected.\n5. *Reconnect traffic to services and monitor*\"\n176
